title: stat /home/USER1/.config/containers/storage.conf: permission denied"
body:
Any idea of what causing this?

`stat /home/USER1/.config/containers/storage.conf: permission denied"`

Actually I started the container as USER 2 and have no config for that user, so why podman is trying to `stat`
 into other USER directory.

comments.body: Did you copy any configurations from the first user? Or, potentially, do a `sudo` or `su` from the other user (these will retain environment variables from the first user in some cases, so we get a bad XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, for example)?
Usually this is $HOME or $XDG_* directories pointing at the previous users information, when you su or sudo to a different user.

You’ll need to set them in your shell before calling ‘podman exec’.

On Sat, Jun 19, 2021 at 05:28 statiksof ***@***.***> wrote:

> @rhatdan <https://github.com/rhatdan> @mheon <https://github.com/mheon>
> thank you for your quick replies. Is there a way to set those variables in
> real time? I mean something like:
>
> podman exec --env XDG_=...
>
> I am already using --root, it solved me other issues but still having this
> one.
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
>
>
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/10725#issuecomment-864381726>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCFUKSQAV6XLGOUINHLTTRPM3ANCNFSM465ZWQIA>
> .
>
@statiksof, make sure to use the `--login` flag when using sudo or su.  That will setup the required login session.
